docs.text: SceneShift Documentation SceneShift is a terminal-based utility for managing application states on Windows. It helps you reclaim system resources quickly by switching your PC into predefined scenes — focused system states tailored for work, gaming, streaming, or recovery. SceneShift is opinionated by design: fast execution, minimal UI, and explicit control. What SceneShift Solves Modern Windows workflows accumulate background apps silently. Launchers, updaters, browsers, sync tools — all consuming RAM and CPU. SceneShift exists to: - reduce friction before performance-critical tasks - replace manual task-killing rituals - make system state management repeatable Core Concepts Scenes A scene is a named preset that defines which applications should be suspended or terminated. Modes - Kill: force-close selected processes - Suspend: pause processes without terminating them Snapshots Capture system memory state before and after a scene switch. Start Here If you're new: → Go to Getting Started
docs.title: Home

docs.text: Community & Contribution SceneShift exists because a personal pain became shared. Feedback Users have shaped: - Preset behavior - Safety defaults - Documentation clarity Feedback happens on GitHub. How You Can Help Share presets Report bugs Improve documentation Star the repository Supporting the Project SceneShift is open source. If it saves you time or frustration: - Sponsor via Ko-fi https://ko-fi.com/tandukuda - Donate via PayPal https://paypal.me/justbams Support keeps the project alive.

docs.text: Configuration Config files are stored in the executable directory: SceneShift/ ├── SceneShift.exe ├── config.yaml # Apps, presets, safelist, keybindings └── theme.yaml # Active theme colors Example config.yaml hotkeys: up: [up, k] down: [down, j] kill_mode: [K] suspend_mode: [S] # NEW in v2.1 resume_mode: [U] # NEW in v2.1 apps: - name: Discord process_name: Discord.exe exec_path: C:\Users\...\Discord.exe selected: false presets: - name: Gaming Mode key: "1" apps: [Discord, Chrome, Spotify] safelist: # NEW in v2.1 - explorer.exe - dwm.exe - csrss.exe # ... prevents killing these 🎨 Themes Built-in themes: - Rose Pine Moon (default) - Dracula - Nord - Gruvbox Dark - Cyberpunk Press t to switch, e to edit colors in real-time.
docs.title: Configuration

docs.title: 🎨 Themes

docs.text: Getting Started SceneShift runs as a portable Windows executable. Requirements Windows 10 or newer Administrator privileges (required for process control) Installation Download the latest release from GitHub Extract the archive Run SceneShift.exe as Administrator No installer. No background services. First Launch On first launch: - SceneShift creates its config directory - Default scenes are generated - You can immediately switch scenes SceneShift does nothing automatically. All actions are explicit. Safety Note Kill mode terminates applications instantly. Save your work before switching scenes.
docs.title: Getting Started

docs.text: Installation SceneShift is built for Windows 10 and 11 . Method 1: Scoop (Recommended) The easiest way to install and keep SceneShift updated is using the Scoop package manager. Open PowerShell and run: # 1. Add the bucket scoop bucket add tandukuda https://github.com/tandukuda/scoop-bucket # 2. Install SceneShift scoop install sceneshift Method 2: Manual Installation If you prefer not to use a package manager, you can run the standalone binary. Download: Get the latest release from the Releases Page . Organize: Create a new folder (e.g., Documents/SceneShift ) and move SceneShift.exe inside. !!! warning "Important" Do not run the app directly from your Desktop or Downloads! On the first run, SceneShift automatically generates config.yaml and theme.yaml . Keeping it in a separate folder ensures your files stay organized. Run: Double-click SceneShift.exe . Note: Windows will request Administrator access to manage processes. Shortcut: Right-click SceneShift.exe → Send to → Desktop (create shortcut) for quick access later. Method 3: Build From Source If you prefer to build it yourself, you will need Go 1.21+ installed. # 1. Clone the repo git clone https://github.com/tandukuda/SceneShift.git cd SceneShift # 2. Install resource tool (for icon embedding) go install github.com/akavel/rsrc@latest # 3. Build with assets rsrc -manifest sceneshift.manifest -ico assets/icon.ico -o sceneshift.syso go build -ldflags "-s -w" -o SceneShift.exe
docs.title: Getting Started

docs.text: Safety & Responsibility SceneShift is a power tool. What SceneShift Can Do Terminate running processes Suspend system applications These actions are irreversible per session. Best Practices Save work before switching scenes Avoid killing system-critical processes Test new presets carefully SceneShift will not protect you from bad decisions.
docs.title: Safety & Disclaimer

docs.title: Safety & Responsibility

docs.text: Troubleshooting Scene doesn't work Ensure SceneShift is running as Administrator Check process names carefully App closes unexpectedly You may have included it in a kill scene Review your preset configuration Nothing happens Some system processes cannot be controlled This is expected behavior Still stuck? Open a GitHub issue with: - SceneShift version - Scene used - Expected behavior
docs.title: Troubleshooting

docs.text: Ensure SceneShift is running as Administrator Check process names carefully
docs.title: Scene doesn't work

docs.text: You may have included it in a kill scene Review your preset configuration
docs.title: App closes unexpectedly

docs.text: Some system processes cannot be controlled This is expected behavior
docs.title: Nothing happens

docs.title: Still stuck?

docs.text: Usage Guide SceneShift is keyboard-centric. You can navigate the interface entirely without a mouse. Basic Commands # Run the TUI SceneShift.exe # Show version SceneShift.exe --version # Show help SceneShift.exe --help Keybindings Main Menu Key Action ↑ / k Move up ↓ / j Move down Space Toggle selection a Select all x Deselect all Actions Key Action Description K Kill Terminate selected processes S Suspend Freeze processes (keeps state) 🆕 U Resume Unfreeze suspended processes 🆕 R Launch Start/restart applications Management Key Action n New app entry e Edit selected d Delete selected p Manage presets t Change theme w Manage safelist 🆕 Ctrl+F Search running processes ? Toggle help q Quit 💡 Example Workflows Gaming Mode 1. Select: Discord, Chrome, Spotify, Steam (when not gaming) 2. Press 'S' to suspend them all 3. Play your game with max performance 4. Press 'U' after gaming to resume 5. Everything's back exactly as you left it! Work Focus 1. Create preset: "Deep Work" (kills Slack, Discord, Email) 2. Assign hotkey: '1' 3. Press '1' in menu → Instant focus mode 4. Press 'R' when done → Restore communication apps Before Streaming 1. Preset: "Stream Mode" 2. Kills: Torrent clients, auto-updaters, notification daemons 3. One hotkey press → Clean system for streaming
docs.title: Usage
